paper.title: Blue extreme disk-runaway stars with Gaia EDR3

extraction.summary: Extracted 5 Galactic-unbound HVS candidates from the sample of 30 extreme blue disk-runaway stars: S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, LAMOST HVS 1, LAMOST HVS 4. These are the objects the paper explicitly identifies as gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Gaia EDR3 astrometry (positions, proper motions, parallaxes) for the initial sample of stars.

source_refs.context: With proper motions of significantly improved accuracy and precision from the Gaia early data release 3 (EDR3)

method_chain.summary: Radial velocities derived from model atmosphere fitting to optical spectra from various instruments (MMT, LAMOST, UVES, etc.).

source_refs.context: radial velocity vrad were determined by fitting ... model spectra

method_chain.summary: Atmospheric parameters (Teff, log(g), vsin(i)) determined from model atmosphere fitting to optical spectra.

source_refs.context: Atmospheric parameters such as effective temperature Teff, surface gravity log(g), projected rotational velocity vsin(i) ... were determined by fitting ...

method_chain.summary: Synthetic SED fitting to observed UV, optical, and IR magnitudes to derive angular diameter and interstellar extinction.

source_refs.context: a synthetic spectral energy distribution that was fit to measured magnitudes ... in order to derive the angular diameter Theta of the star and the interstellar extinction.

method_chain.summary: Stellar masses and ages inferred by comparing position in Kiel diagram to Geneva evolutionary tracks (Ekström et al. 2012; Georgy et al. 2013).

source_refs.context: Stellar masses M and ages tau_evol were inferred by comparing the position of a star in the Kiel diagram to evolutionary tracks by the Geneva group

method_chain.summary: Spectrophotometric distance derived from surface gravity, mass, radius, and angular diameter.

source_refs.context: The spectrophotometric distance d finally followed from solving the definition of the surface gravity ... for the stellar radius R and inserting the latter in the definition of the angular diameter

method_chain.summary: Solar position and motion adopted from Schönrich et al. (2010). The paper does not report the actual numeric values (R0, z0, v_circ_sun, U, V, W).

parameters.name: R0
parameters.description: Distance to the Galactic Center, not explicitly stated

source_refs.context: Uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest (Schönrich et al. 2010) are treated analogously.

parameters.name: z0
parameters.description: Solar height above the Galactic plane, not stated

parameters.name: v_circ_sun
parameters.description: Circular velocity at the Sun, not stated

parameters.name: solar_motion_u
parameters.description: Solar peculiar motion U, not stated

parameters.name: solar_motion_v
parameters.description: Solar peculiar motion V, not stated

parameters.name: solar_motion_w
parameters.description: Solar peculiar motion W, not stated

source_refs.context: Uncertainties in the distance of the Sun to the GC and in its relative motion to the local standard of rest (Schönrich et al. 2010) are treated analogously.

method_chain.summary: Transformation of observed equatorial coordinates, distances, proper motions, and radial velocities to Galactocentric Cartesian positions and velocities, and computation of Galactic rest-frame velocities.

source_refs.context: spectrophotometric distances and radial velocities were combined with proper motions from Gaia EDR3 to trace back the trajectories ... to derive their ejection velocities ... and to identify their spatial origins

method_chain.summary: Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013), a three-component Galactic potential (spherical bulge, axisymmetric disk, spherical dark matter halo).

parameters.name: potential_name
parameters.raw_value: Model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)
parameters.value: Model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)

source_refs.context: we used the Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)

parameters.name: escape_velocity_definition
parameters.raw_value: local Galactic escape velocity from the model potential
parameters.value: local Galactic escape velocity from the potential

source_refs.context: we used the Milky Way mass model I of Irrgang et al. (2013)

method_chain.summary: Backward integration of orbits using a fourth-order Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg method with adaptive step-size control, Monte Carlo simulation with 10^6 runs to propagate uncertainties.

source_refs.context: The corresponding equations of motions are numerically solved with a fourth-order Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg method ... Error propagation ... is handled with a Monte Carlo simulation with one million runs

method_chain.summary: Computation of local Galactic escape velocity, Galactic rest-frame velocity, escape velocity margin (v_Grf - v_esc), and bound probability P_b from the Monte Carlo runs.

source_refs.context: the local Galactic escape velocity v_esc ... The probability P_b is the fraction of Monte Carlo runs for which the star is bound to the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Derivation of ejection velocity v_ej (Galactic rest-frame velocity relative to the rotating disk) and galactocentric radius at plane intersection r_p.

source_refs.context: ejection velocities v_ej ... galactocentric radii at plane intersection r_p

method_chain.summary: Selection of the final 30 program stars based on mass-dependent ejection velocity threshold (400 km/s for M<5 Msun, 320 km/s for M>5 Msun) and well-constrained disk origin (r_p < 25 kpc).

source_refs.context: Target selection: Based on proper motions from Gaia EDR3, we then carried out spectroscopic and kinematic analyses ... to filter out those with both a high ejection velocity and a relatively well-constrained origin within the Galactic disk.
a mass-dependent threshold for the deduced 1σ upper limit of the ejection velocity, that is, 400 km/s or 320 km/s for stars with masses below or above 5 Msun

inclusion_assessment.summary: S5-HVS 1 is the most extreme object in the sample with v_ej = 1810 km/s. The paper states that it is the only object among the three GC candidates that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way. P_b = 0.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Clear textual statement: 'It is also the only object that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way' and P_b=0.

source_refs.context: S5-HVS 1 ... is also the only object that is gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way

However, overwhelming observational evidence for the operation of the Hills mechanism has also been found by Koposov et al. (2020). They discovered that the A-type star S5-HVS 1 travels at ... and most likely originates in the GC.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 5 is explicitly described as 'clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way'. P_b = 0.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper states: 'HVS 5 is certainly one of the most interesting targets because it is ... clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way' and P_b=0.

source_refs.context: HVS 5 is certainly ... clearly gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way
14 stars from the MMT-HVS survey ... were included

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 6 is explicitly identified as gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way. P_b = 0.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper states: 'HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way'. P_b=0 in kinematic table.

source_refs.context: HVS 6 is unbound to the Milky Way
14 stars from the MMT-HVS survey ... were included

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 1 is classified as a hyper-runaway star, indicating it is unbound to the Milky Way. P_b = 2 %.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly describes LAMOST HVS 1 as a 'hyper-runaway star', defined as a star unbound from the Galaxy. P_b=2 % supports unbound status.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars.
the four candidate HVSs from the LAMOST survey

inclusion_assessment.summary: LAMOST HVS 4 is classified as a hyper-runaway star, indicating it is unbound to the Milky Way. P_b = 0.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly groups LAMOST HVS 4 with hyper-runaway stars (unbound). P_b=0 confirms unbound.

source_refs.context: LAMOST HVS 1 and LAMOST HVS 4 are two further examples for such so-called hyper-runaway stars.
the four candidate HVSs from the LAMOST survey

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 30 extreme blue disk-runaway stars listed in Table 1 of the paper.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Five stars are identified as gravitationally unbound to the Milky Way (S5-HVS 1, HVS 5, HVS 6, LAMOST HVS 1, LAMOST HVS 4) and are extracted as HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: Table 1 listing the 30 program stars
